<task id="42.4-15" type="framework" status="planned"><goal>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape — readers should never manually combine them. Add typed interface, merge function, and tests for: (a) species inherits all project fields when species.json is empty, (b) species overrides individual fields cleanly, (c) species can add species-only fields without affecting project defaults, (d) `inherits_from` resolves a chain across species within the same project. Decision forge-10 captures the rationale. This is what the forge editor's "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" UX is built on.</goal>
      <keywords>schema,inheritance,project-species,merge,loader,forge-10</keywords>
    </task>
inputs.new_string:
<task id="42.4-15" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape — readers should never manually combine them. Shipped: new `lib/eval-loader.cjs` exporting `loadProject`, `loadSpecies`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics per decision gad-184: project defaults → inherits_from chain (deepest ancestor first) → target species; last write wins; arrays REPLACE not merge; species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) do not back-propagate to project; cycles throw. Wired `bin/gad.cjs eval list` to the loader and swapped `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` to use `loadResolvedSpecies` so the site sees the merged shape. Hermetic tests in `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` cover (a)-(e) plus loadAllResolvedSpecies + pure-function spot check (7/7 pass via `node --test`). Other legacy `gad.json` reads in bin/gad.cjs (eval run, eval verify, eval review, eval open/report) are orthogonal rename fallout and left for a separate surgical pass.</goal>
      <keywords>schema,inheritance,project-species,merge,loader,forge-10,gad-184</keywords>
    </task>

inputs.old_string:
<decision id="gad-183">
    <title>Proto-skills stage inside the owning project's `.planning/` and install outward into runtimes on demand</title>
    <summary>Proto-skills are not canonical framework assets yet; they are project-local planning artifacts awaiting human review. Policy: the canonical staging directory for proto-skills is `.planning/proto-skills/` inside the owning GAD project, not `skills/proto-skills/`. This keeps drafted proto-skills in the same lifecycle zone as tasks, decisions, snapshots, and workflows until the operator explicitly decides what to do next. From that staged location there are now two explicit outward moves: `gad evolution install <slug>` installs the staged proto-skill into one or more coding-agent runtimes for local testing while keeping it staged; `gad evolution promote <slug>` promotes the staged proto-skill into canonical `skills/` so it joins the framework DNA. Promotion must preserve provenance rather than dropping it.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Live evolution CLI paths move from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/`. (2) `gad evolution install` becomes the fast bridge from planning artifact to runtime test surface, mirroring the existing runtime install conventions (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`, local/global, optional config dir). (3) `gad evolution promote` now targets canonical `skills/` and carries the full staged bundle forward, including `PROVENANCE.md`. (4) Candidate generation and proto-skill docs must stop describing proto-skills as a subdirectory of the public skill catalog. (5) Historical candidate/eval artifacts may still mention the old path, but canonical source, CLI help, and planning docs should point at `.planning/proto-skills/`.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>
inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-183">
    <title>Proto-skills stage inside the owning project's `.planning/` and install outward into runtimes on demand</title>
    <summary>Proto-skills are not canonical framework assets yet; they are project-local planning artifacts awaiting human review. Policy: the canonical staging directory for proto-skills is `.planning/proto-skills/` inside the owning GAD project, not `skills/proto-skills/`. This keeps drafted proto-skills in the same lifecycle zone as tasks, decisions, snapshots, and workflows until the operator explicitly decides what to do next. From that staged location there are now two explicit outward moves: `gad evolution install <slug>` installs the staged proto-skill into one or more coding-agent runtimes for local testing while keeping it staged; `gad evolution promote <slug>` promotes the staged proto-skill into canonical `skills/` so it joins the framework DNA. Promotion must preserve provenance rather than dropping it.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Live evolution CLI paths move from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/`. (2) `gad evolution install` becomes the fast bridge from planning artifact to runtime test surface, mirroring the existing runtime install conventions (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`, local/global, optional config dir). (3) `gad evolution promote` now targets canonical `skills/` and carries the full staged bundle forward, including `PROVENANCE.md`. (4) Candidate generation and proto-skill docs must stop describing proto-skills as a subdirectory of the public skill catalog. (5) Historical candidate/eval artifacts may still mention the old path, but canonical source, CLI help, and planning docs should point at `.planning/proto-skills/`.</impact>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-184">
    <title>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract: merge-at-read-time eval loader</title>
    <summary>Every eval project has one `evals/<project>/project.json` declaring defaults (`techStack`, `co
... [truncated 552 bytes] ...
es`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, and `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics are: (1) start from project.json as base; (2) walk `inherits_from` deepest-ancestor first, applying each species on top; (3) apply target species last with last-write-wins; (4) array fields REPLACE, never concat (a future `_extends` convention could opt into merging but is out of scope); (5) species-only fields stay on the resolved shape and never back-propagate to the shared project defaults; (6) cycles throw a clear `inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a` error; (7) unknown parent references throw. Cross-references forge-10 which captured the editor-UX rationale (separate "edit project" vs "edit species" surfaces demand a single canonical merger).</summary>
    <impact>(1) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` is the canonical location for project-species composition; adding or changing merge rules is one file, one function. (2) `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` now route through the loader, replacing hand-rolled project.json + species.json combinations. (3) Hermetic inheritance tests live at `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` and lock the contract (5 required cases + 2 smoke checks, all green via `node --test`). (4) The forge editor can safely present "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" as separate surfaces: whatever the operator edits at each level round-trips through the same merge on reload. (5) Other legacy `gad.json` reads inside `bin/gad.cjs` (eval run metadata, eval verify, eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14 and should migrate to the loader in a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep 42.4-15 scoped. (6) Arrays replacing (not concatenating) is a load-bearing choice: callers who want to "extend" a project-level skill list must spell out the full species list explicitly until an `_extends` convention is designed.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>

inputs.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(framework): project ⊇ species inheritance contract in eval loader (42.4-15)

Add lib/eval-loader.cjs — the canonical merger for evals/<project>/project.json
+ evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json. Exports loadProject,
loadSpecies, loadAllSpeciesRaw, loadResolvedSpecies, loadAllResolvedSpecies,
mergeProjectSpecies, resolveInheritanceChain.

Merge semantics (decision gad-184):
  1. project.json defaults are the base layer
  2. inherits_from chain walked deepest-ancestor first
  3. target species applied last, last write wins
  4. array fields REPLACE, not concat (no _extends convention yet)
  5. species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) stay local and
     never back-propagate to project defaults
  6. cycles throw a clear 'inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a' error

Wire bin/gad.cjs `eval list` through loadEvalProject + loadAllResolvedSpecies
instead of hand-reading gad.json (which no longer exists after 42.4-14). The
site prebuild scanEvalProjects path was already updated in a prior piggybacked
commit (32055ad) and is left as-is.

Hermetic tests at tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs cover all five
required cases (a-e) plus loadAllResolvedSpecies + mergeProjectSpecies purity
spot checks — 7/7 pass under `node --test`.

Updates TASK-REGISTRY 42.4-15 to done, STATE next-action, DECISIONS gad-184.

Other legacy gad.json reads inside bin/gad.cjs (eval run metadata, eval verify,
eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14
and left for a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep this
change scoped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
